{"components":[{"internalType":"address","name":"addr","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint48","name":"expiration","type":"uint48"}],"internalType":"struct ENSValidator.Owner[]","name":"ownersToAdd","type":"tuple[]"},{"internalType":"address[]","name":"ownersToRemove","type":"address[]"}],"name":"updateConfig","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"owner","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint48","name":"newExpiration","type":"uint48"}],"name":"updateOwnerExpiration","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"hash","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"signature","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"data","type":"bytes"}],"name":"validateSignatureWithData","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"components":[{"internalType":"address","name":"sender","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"nonce","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"initCode","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"callData","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"accountGasLimits","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"preVerificationGas","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"gasFees","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"paymasterAndData","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"signature","type":"bytes"}],"internalType":"struct PackedUserOperation","name":"userOp","type":"tuple"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"userOpHash","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"validateUserOp","outputs":[{"internalType":"ERC7579ValidatorBase.ValidationData","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"version","outputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"","type":"string"}],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"custom:security":"This contract implements several security measures: - Signature uniqueness: Prevents signature reuse by sorting and uniquifying recovered signers - Expiration checks: Automatically invalidates expired owners during validation - Invariant checks: Ensures threshold is always valid relative to owner count - Owner count limits: Enforces MIN_OWNERS (1) and MAX_OWNERS (32) constraints","details":"This contract provides both stateful and stateless signature validation modes for smart accounts.      It extends ERC7579ValidatorBase to provide multi-signature validation with a configurable threshold      and time-based owner expiration functionality. Key Features: - Multi-signature validation with configurable threshold (k-of-n signatures required) - Time-based owner expiration mechanism for automatic access revocation - Dual validation modes: stateful (using stored config) and stateless (config passed with signature) - EIP-1271 signature validation support for off-chain signature verification - Per-account owner management using EnumerableSet for gas-efficient storage Owner Storage: Owners are stored as packed bytes32 values in an EnumerableSet, where each element contains: - Owner address (160 bits) - Expiration timestamp (48 bits) - type(uint48).max means permanent/no expiration - Unused space (48 bits) This packing strategy optimizes storage costs while maintaining efficient lookups. Validation Modes: 1. Stateful (validateUserOp, isValidSignatureWithSender): Uses stored threshold and owners 2. Stateless (validateSignatureWithData): Accepts threshold and owners as calldata, useful for    signature validation before module installation or for off-chain verification","errors":{"ExpirationInPast(uint48)":[{"params":{"expiration":"The invalid expiration timestamp"}}],"InvalidOwnersCount(uint256,uint256,uint256)":[{"params":{"maxOwnersCount":"Maximum allowed owners (MAX_OWNERS)","minOwnersCount":"Minimum allowed owners (MIN_OWNERS)","ownersCount":"The invalid owner count"}}],"InvalidThreshold(uint256,uint256,uint256)":[{"params":{"maxThreshold":"Maximum allowed threshold (usually current owner count)","minThreshold":"Minimum allowed threshold (usually MIN_OWNERS or threshold lower bound)","threshold":"The invalid threshold value"}}]},"events":{"ModuleInitialized(address)":{"params":{"account":"The smart account address that installed this module"}},"ModuleUninitialized(address)":{"params":{"account":"The smart account address that uninstalled this module"}},"OwnerAdded(address,address)":{"params":{"account":"The smart account address","owner":"The address of the added owner"}},"OwnerExpirationUpdated(address,address,uint48)":{"params":{"account":"The smart account address","newExpiration":"The new expiration timestamp","owner":"The address of the owner whose expiration was updated"}},"OwnerRemoved(address,address)":{"params":{"account":"The smart account address","owner":"The address of the removed owner"}},"ThresholdSet(address,uint256)":{"params":{"account":"The smart account address","threshold":"The new signature threshold (number of signatures required)"}}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"getOwnerExpiration(address,address)":{"details":"Returns 0 for non-existent owners. For existing owners, type(uint48).max means permanent.      Callers should use isOwner to check existence first if needed.","params":{"account":"The account to check","owner":"The owner address to get expiration for"},"returns":{"_0":"uint48 The expiration timestamp (0 if owner doesn't exist, type(uint48).max = permanent/never expires)"}},"getOwners(address)":{"custom:gas":"O(n) where n is number of owners. Loads all owner data from storage.","details":"Returns ALL owners including expired ones. Callers should check expiration      if they need only currently valid owners. This function unpacks the bytes32      storage elements into human-readable Owner structs.","params":{"account":"The account to get owners for"},"returns":{"ownersArray":"Array of Owner structs containing addresses and expiration times"}},"getOwnersCount(address)":{"details":"This count includes expired owners since expiration doesn't remove them from storage","params":{"account":"The account to get owner count for"},"returns":{"_0":"uint256 The number of owners (including expired owners)"}},"isInitialized(address)":{"details":"Initialization state is determined by non-zero threshold since threshold      is set during onInstall and reset to 0 during onUninstall","params":{"smartAccount":"The account address to check"},"returns":{"_0":"bool True if initialized (threshold > 0), false otherwise"}},"isModuleType(uint256)":{"details":"This module supports both TYPE_VALIDATOR and TYPE_STATELESS_VALIDATOR:      - TYPE_VALIDATOR: Standard stateful validation using stored configuration      - TYPE_STATELESS_VALIDATOR: Validation with configuration passed in calldata      The dual-mode support allows this module to validate signatures even before      it's installed on an account (useful for signature verification workflows)","params":{"typeID":"The module type identifier to check"},"returns":{"_0":"bool True if the module type is supported, false otherwise"}},"isOwner(address,address)":{"details":"This only checks existence, NOT validity. An expired owner will still return true.      For validity checking (including expiration), use _isOwnerValid internally or      check getOwnerExpiration and compare with block.timestamp","params":{"account":"The account to check","owner":"The address to check for ownership"},"returns":{"_0":"bool True if the address is an owner (regardless of expiration), false otherwise"}},"isValidSignatureWithSender(address,bytes32,bytes)":{"custom:security":"Unlike validateUserOp, this uses the RAW hash without EIP-191 prefix                  This is correct for EIP-712 signatures which include their own domain separator                  msg.sender (the account) is used for config lookup","details":"This function enables the smart account to validate off-chain signatures      It uses the raw hash without EIP-191 prefix, suitable for EIP-712 typed data","params":{"data":"The signature data to validate","hash":"The hash of the data that was signed (typically an EIP-712 hash)"},"returns":{"_0":"bytes4 EIP1271_SUCCESS (0x1626ba7e) if valid, EIP1271_FAILED (0xffffffff) otherwise"}},"name()":{"details":"Note: This returns \"OwnableValidator\" for compatibility but this is actually      the ENSValidator with expiration support","returns":{"_0":"string The module name"}},"onInstall(bytes)":{"custom:security":"The moduleIsNotInitialized modifier prevents re-initialization                  The ensureValidConfig modifier ensures valid initial configuration","details":"Called by the smart account during module installation      Decodes initialization data to set up initial owners and threshold","params":{"data":"ABI-encoded tuple of (uint256 threshold, Owner[] owners)"}},"onUninstall(bytes)":{"custom:security":"After uninstall, checkInvariants is called with (0,0) bounds to ensure                  complete cleanup. The account will have 0 owners and 0 threshold.","details":"Removes all owners and resets threshold to 0"},"updateConfig(uint256,(address,uint48)[],address[])":{"custom:security":"The ensureValidConfig modifier ensures the final configuration is valid                  Callers should ensure they're not removing critical owners or setting                  an unreachable threshold","details":"This function allows atomic updates to threshold and owner set      WARNING: This function is NOT idempotent - calling it multiple times with the      same parameters may have different effects (e.g., if owners expire between calls)","params":{"newThreshold":"The new signature threshold (must satisfy: MIN_OWNERS <= threshold <= owner_count)","ownersToAdd":"Array of owners to add with their expiration timestamps (type(uint48).max = permanent/never expires)","ownersToRemove":"Array of owner addresses to remove from the account"}},"updateOwnerExpiration(address,uint48)":{"custom:gas":"This performs a remove + add operation (2 SSTOREs) rather than an in-place update             because EnumerableSet doesn't support element modification","custom:security":"Validates that new expiration is either permanent or in the future","details":"Useful for extending or reducing an owner's access period without removing and re-adding      The owner must already exist in the set","params":{"newExpiration":"The new expiration timestamp (type(uint48).max = permanent/never expires)","owner":"The address of the existing owner to update"}},"validateSignatureWithData(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"custom:security":"This function has important security properties:                  1. Owners array MUST be sorted and unique (prevents owner reuse)                  2. Recovered signers are sorted and uniquified (prevents signature reuse)                  3. Threshold must be > 0 (prevents trivial bypass)                  4. Uses binary search for efficient owner lookup                  5. Does NOT check expiration (by design, as expiration requires stored config)","details":"This is a stateless validation function that doesn't rely on stored configuration      Useful for validating signatures before module installation or for off-chain verification      WARNING: This function does NOT check owner expiration - all provided owners are treated as valid","params":{"data":"ABI-encoded tuple of (uint256 threshold, address[] owners)","hash":"The hash of the data that was signed","signature":"The concatenated signatures from the owners"},"returns":{"_0":"bool True if threshold is met by valid owner signatures, false otherwise"}},"validateUserOp((address,uint256,bytes,bytes,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes),bytes32)":{"custom:security":"The userOpHash is wrapped with EIP-191 \"\\x19Ethereum Signed Message\" prefix                  before validation. This prevents signature reuse across different contexts.                  Only signatures from valid (non-expired) owners count toward threshold.","details":"This is the primary validation method for UserOperations in ERC-4337 Account Abstraction      The function performs stateful validation using stored threshold and owners","params":{"userOp":"The packed user operation containing sender, nonce, calldata, and signature","userOpHash":"The hash of the user operation (excluding signature)"},"returns":{"_0":"ValidationData ERC-4337 validation result (VALIDATION_SUCCESS or VALIDATION_FAILED)"}},"version()":{"returns":{"_0":"string The semantic version string"}}},"stateVariables":{"MAX_OWNERS":{"details":"Set to 32 to balance security (enough signers for decentralization) with gas costs      (iteration over owners in validation). This limit prevents DoS via excessive owners."},"MIN_OWNERS":{"details":"Set to 1 to ensure at least one owner exists for account recovery and operations"},"owners":{"details":"Stores packed owner data (address + expiration) for all accounts      Uses EnumerableSet.Bytes32Set where each bytes32 element contains:      - bits 256-97: owner address (160 bits)      - bits 96-49: expiration timestamp (48 bits)      - bits 48-1: unused (48 bits)      The set is namespaced per account address for efficient per-account iteration"},"thresholds":{"details":"A threshold of 0 indicates the module is not initialized for that account      Valid thresholds must satisfy: MIN_OWNERS <= threshold <= owner_count"}},"title":"ENSValidator","version":1},"userdoc":{"errors":{"ExpirationInPast(uint48)":[{"notice":"Thrown when expiration timestamp is in the past (excluding type(uint48).max for permanent)"}],"InvalidOwnersCount(uint256,uint256,uint256)":[{"notice":"Thrown when owner count is outside allowed bounds"}],"InvalidThreshold(uint256,uint256,uint256)":[{"notice":"Thrown when threshold value is invalid relative to owner count"}],"ZeroAddressNotAllowed()":[{"notice":"Thrown when attempting to add the zero address as an owner"}]},"events":{"ModuleInitialized(address)":{"notice":"Emitted when the module is installed for an account"},"ModuleUninitialized(address)":{"notice":"Emitted when the module is uninstalled from an account"},"OwnerAdded(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when an owner is added to an account"},"OwnerExpirationUpdated(address,address,uint48)":{"notice":"Emitted when an owner's expiration timestamp is updated"},"OwnerRemoved(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when an owner is removed from an account"},"ThresholdSet(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when the signature threshold is updated for an account"}},"kind":"user","methods":{"getOwnerExpiration(address,address)":{"notice":"Gets the expiration timestamp for a specific owner"},"getOwners(address)":{"notice":"Retrieves all owners for an account with their expiration timestamps"},"getOwnersCount(address)":{"notice":"Returns the total number of owners for an account"},"isInitialized(address)":{"notice":"Checks if the module is initialized for a given smart account"},"isModuleType(uint256)":{"notice":"Checks if this module supports a given ERC-7579 module type"},"isOwner(address,address)":{"notice":"Checks if an address is an owner of an account"},"isValidSignatureWithSender(address,bytes32,bytes)":{"notice":"Validates an EIP-1271 signature with the sender context"},"name()":{"notice":"Returns the human-readable name of this module"},"onInstall(bytes)":{"notice":"Initializes the module for a smart account"},"onUninstall(bytes)":{"notice":"Uninstalls the module from a smart account"},"thresholds(address)":{"notice":"Maps each account to its required signature threshold"},"updateConfig(uint256,(address,uint48)[],address[])":{"notice":"Updates the configuration for the calling account"},"updateOwnerExpiration(address,uint48)":{"notice":"Updates the expiration time for an existing owner"},"validateSignatureWithData(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"Validates a signature using configuration passed as calldata (stateless mode)"},"validateUserOp((address,uint256,bytes,bytes,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes),bytes32)":{"notice":"Validates a user operation according to ERC-4337 specification"},"version()":{"notice":"Returns the version of this module"}},"notice":"ERC7579 validator module implementing multi-signature validation with time-based owner getOwnerExpiration(address,address).details: Returns 0 for non-existent owners. For existing owners, type(uint48).max means permanent.      Callers should use isOwner to check existence first if needed.

params.owner: The owner address to get expiration for

returns._0: uint48 The expiration timestamp (0 if owner doesn't exist, type(uint48).max = permanent/never expires)

getOwners(address).custom:gas: O(n) where n is number of owners. Loads all owner data from storage.
getOwners(address).details: Returns ALL owners including expired ones. Callers should check expiration      if they need only currently valid owners. This function unpacks the bytes32      storage elements into human-readable Owner structs.

params.account: The account to get owners for

returns.ownersArray: Array of Owner structs containing addresses and expiration times

getOwnersCount(address).details: This count includes expired owners since expiration doesn't remove them from storage

params.account: The account to get owner count for

returns._0: uint256 The number of owners (including expired owners)

isInitialized(address).details: Initialization state is determined by non-zero threshold since threshold      is set during onInstall and reset to 0 during onUninstall

returns._0: bool True if initialized (threshold > 0), false otherwise

isModuleType(uint256).details: This module supports both TYPE_VALIDATOR and TYPE_STATELESS_VALIDATOR:      - TYPE_VALIDATOR: Standard stateful validation using stored configuration      - TYPE_STATELESS_VALIDATOR: Validation with configuration passed in calldata      The dual-mode support allows this module to validate signatures even before      it's installed on an account (useful for signature verification workflows)

params.typeID: The module type identifier to check

returns._0: bool True if the module type is supported, false otherwise

isOwner(address,address).details: This only checks existence, NOT validity. An expired owner will still return true.      For validity checking (including expiration), use _isOwnerValid internally or      check getOwnerExpiration and compare with block.timestamp

params.owner: The address to check for ownership

returns._0: bool True if the address is an owner (regardless of expiration), false otherwise

isValidSignatureWithSender(address,bytes.custom:security: Unlike validateUserOp, this uses the RAW hash without EIP-191 prefix                  This is correct for EIP-712 signatures which include their own domain separator                  msg.sender (the account) is used for config lookup
isValidSignatureWithSender(address,bytes.details: This function enables the smart account to validate off-chain signatures      It uses the raw hash without EIP-191 prefix, suitable for EIP-712 typed data

params.hash: The hash of the data that was signed (typically an EIP-712 hash)

returns._0: bytes4 EIP1271_SUCCESS (0x1626ba7e) if valid, EIP1271_FAILED (0xffffffff) otherwise

name().details: Note: This returns "OwnableValidator" for compatibility but this is actually      the ENSValidator with expiration support

onInstall(bytes).custom:security: The moduleIsNotInitialized modifier prevents re-initialization                  The ensureValidConfig modifier ensures valid initial configuration
onInstall(bytes).details: Called by the smart account during module installation      Decodes initialization data to set up initial owners and threshold

params.data: ABI-encoded tuple of (uint256 threshold, Owner[] owners)

onUninstall(bytes).custom:security: After uninstall, checkInvariants is called with (0,0) bounds to ensure                  complete cleanup. The account will have 0 owners and 0 threshold.
onUninstall(bytes).details: Removes all owners and resets threshold to 0

updateConfig(uint256,(address,uint48)[],.custom:security: The ensureValidConfig modifier ensures the final configuration is valid                  Callers should ensure they're not removing critical owners or setting                  an unreachable threshold
updateConfig(uint256,(address,uint48)[],.details: This function allows atomic updates to threshold and owner set      WARNING: This function is NOT idempotent - calling it multiple times with the      same parameters may have different effects (e.g., if owners expire between calls)

params.newThreshold: The new signature threshold (must satisfy: MIN_OWNERS <= threshold <= owner_count)
params.ownersToAdd: Array of owners to add with their expiration timestamps (type(uint48).max = permanent/never expires)
params.ownersToRemove: Array of owner addresses to remove from the account

updateOwnerExpiration(address,uint48).custom:gas: This performs a remove + add operation (2 SSTOREs) rather than an in-place update             because EnumerableSet doesn't support element modification
updateOwnerExpiration(address,uint48).custom:security: Validates that new expiration is either permanent or in the future
updateOwnerExpiration(address,uint48).details: Useful for extending or reducing an owner's access period without removing and re-adding      The owner must already exist in the set

params.newExpiration: The new expiration timestamp (type(uint48).max = permanent/never expires)
params.owner: The address of the existing owner to update

validateSignatureWithData(bytes32,bytes,.custom:security: This function has important security properties:                  1. Owners array MUST be sorted and unique (prevents owner reuse)                  2. Recovered signers are sorted and uniquified (prevents signature reuse)                  3. Threshold must be > 0 (prevents trivial bypass)                  4. Uses binary search for efficient owner lookup                  5. Does NOT check expiration (by design, as expiration requires stored config)
validateSignatureWithData(bytes32,bytes,.details: This is a stateless validation function that doesn't rely on stored configuration      Useful for validating signatures before module installation or for off-chain verification      WARNING: This function does NOT check owner expiration - all provided owners are treated as valid

params.data: ABI-encoded tuple of (uint256 threshold, address[] owners)
params.hash: The hash of the data that was signed
params.signature: The concatenated signatures from the owners

returns._0: bool True if threshold is met by valid owner signatures, false otherwise

validateUserOp((address,uint256,bytes,by.custom:security: The userOpHash is wrapped with EIP-191 "\x19Ethereum Signed Message" prefix                  before validation. This prevents signature reuse across different contexts.                  Only signatures from valid (non-expired) owners count toward threshold.
validateUserOp((address,uint256,bytes,by.details: This is the primary validation method for UserOperations in ERC-4337 Account Abstraction      The function performs stateful validation using stored threshold and owners

params.userOp: The packed user operation containing sender, nonce, calldata, and signature
params.userOpHash: The hash of the user operation (excluding signature)

returns._0: ValidationData ERC-4337 validation result (VALIDATION_SUCCESS or VALIDATION_FAILED)

getOwnerExpiration(address,address).notice: Gets the expiration timestamp for a specific owner

getOwners(address).notice: Retrieves all owners for an account with their expiration timestamps

getOwnersCount(address).notice: Returns the total number of owners for an account

isInitialized(address).notice: Checks if the module is initialized for a given smart account

isModuleType(uint256).notice: Checks if this module supports a given ERC-7579 module type

isOwner(address,address).notice: Checks if an address is an owner of an account

isValidSignatureWithSender(address,bytes.notice: Validates an EIP-1271 signature with the sender context

name().notice: Returns the human-readable name of this module

onInstall(bytes).notice: Initializes the module for a smart account

onUninstall(bytes).notice: Uninstalls the module from a smart account

thresholds(address).notice: Maps each account to its required signature threshold

updateConfig(uint256,(address,uint48)[],.notice: Updates the configuration for the calling account

updateOwnerExpiration(address,uint48).notice: Updates the expiration time for an existing owner

validateSignatureWithData(bytes32,bytes,.notice: Validates a signature using configuration passed as calldata (stateless mode)

validateUserOp((address,uint256,bytes,by.notice: Validates a user operation according to ERC-4337 specification

version().notice: Returns the version of this module
